message:
fix(core): enforce PRIMARY KEY uniqueness for non-integer PK columns

Non-integer PRIMARY KEY columns (e.g., TEXT PRIMARY KEY) were not
getting implicit UNIQUE index generation in parse_columns_from_create_sql.
The unique field was gated on `is_ipk && upper.contains("PRIMARY KEY")`,
which is false for TEXT columns since is_ipk requires INTEGER type.
This caused duplicate values to be silently accepted on INSERT.

Fix: simplify to `upper.contains("PRIMARY KEY")` so ALL primary key
columns get unique=true. Downstream consumers already guard IPK columns
with `!col.is_ipk` checks, so INTEGER PRIMARY KEY (rowid alias) handling
is unaffected.

Fixes #9

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -505,7 +505,7 @@ pub fn parse_columns_from_create_sql(sql: &str) -> Vec<ColumnInfo> {
                 is_ipk,
                 type_name,
                 notnull: upper.contains("NOT NULL"),
-                unique: upper.contains("UNIQUE") || (is_ipk && upper.contains("PRIMARY KEY")),
+                unique: upper.contains("UNIQUE") || upper.contains("PRIMARY KEY"),
                 default_value,
                 strict_type,
                 generated_expr: None,
